segments.text:
Rubella (German measles)
CLS-N390 Vaccines: S c ience and Society

Rubella virus
German measles
Single-stranded RNA virus
Pleomorphic nucleocapid envelope
H ost-derived lipid membrane
Same family as Eastern equine encephalitis (EEE), Western equine encephalitis (WEE), Venezuelan equine encephalitis (VEE)
Humans are only reservoir

China 2,202
Mosambique
21.6% of cases
2020 Case Numbers – 10,194
India
Nigeria
Democratic Republic of Congo
65.5% of global cases come from five countries

Transmission
Moderately contagious
Spread up to one week before rash and seen days after
25% - 50% do not develop rash – still spread
Spread via respiratory route – coughing and sneezing
Transmission from mom to baby during pregnancy
R 0 value = 10 – 12; 92% vaccination for herd immunity

Symptoms
Children and Adults
Mild, few symptoms – most common = red rash on face that spreads to rest of body – lasts 3 days
Other symptoms 1 – 5 days before rash:
Low grade fever*
Headache*
Mild pink eye*
General discomfort*
Swollen and enlarged lymph nodes
Cough
Runny nose
Up to 70% of women may experience arthritis

Complications
Most serious complication that can develop
Unvaccinated pregnant women – may lead to miscarriage or death just after birth
Congenital Rubella Syndrome (CRS)
Mom passes virus to baby – more common if infected in first trimester
Heart problems
Loss of hearing and eyesight
Intellectual disabilities
Liver or spleen damage

Rubella Vaccine Timeline
Three vaccines developed:
D uck embryo
D og kidney
R abit kidney
H uman diploid fibroblast strain licensed – all others discontinued
Licensure of measles-mumps-rubella (duck embryo) vaccine
Licensure of measles-mumps-rubella-varicella vaccine
1969
1971
1979
2005
Rubella first isolated in 1962 by Parkman and Weller

Characteristics of Vaccine
Only available as combination vaccine – MMR or MMRV
Efficacy – 95% at 12 months
Immunity – lifelong; 90% show at least 15 year protection
At least one dose needed; two is better
Given to children 12 months or older; others without immunity

Components of Vaccine
Comes as freeze-dried (lyophilized) powder
Live attenuated virus created in human diploid fibroblast tissue culture
Reconstituted with sterile, preservative-free water
Human albumin
Contains small amount of …
Neomycin
Sorbitol
Gelatin

Before 1969 – common and widespread
Eradicated in 2004
less than 10 cases each year (all foreign travelers)
